title: keyfile load does not verify 0600 permissions — silently loads world-readable keys
description: core/src/keyfile.rs:14-33: load_identity reads the file but does not check Unix permissions. If the keyfile is readable by other users (e.g. due to an umask issue or manual copy), the age-encrypted bytes are exposed and a passphrase-less key is immediately compromised. Should check std::os::unix::fs::MetadataExt::mode() & 0o077 == 0 and emit a warning (or error) if the permissions are too broad. This is the same check ssh/gpg perform on startup.
